visual_findings: The round-17 contact sheet showed no hard crop or float displacement; the selected target was page 9, where Discussion, Limitations, and Conclusion share the closing page.
The pre-edit conclusion preserved the correct claim boundary but closed abruptly and rendered the contribution sentence as a compact checklist.

The accepted render uses the available page-9 slack to make the closing claim more reader-facing: conversion changes the evidence seen by a vision-language system and should be reported as a grounding intervention.
The revised second paragraph keeps the same gate list but frames it as an auditable intervention rather than a generic reusable contract.
The after full contact sheet keeps the 11-page structure stable and does not visibly move page 10 Ethical Considerations, References, figures, or tables.
change_summary: Rewrote the first conclusion paragraph to state why material conversion matters for grounding evidence.
Rewrote the closing paragraph around auditable intervention, matched renders, projection QA, prompt/coordinate contracts, material-risk bins, and embodied smoke gates.
Kept the claim boundary that portable assets are useful only for gates they pass.

verification.command: rg -n "Overfull|Float too large|too large|LaTeX Warning: Float|Rerun to get|Warning:.*Label|Undefined references|multiply defined|Package lineno Warning" paper/venues/acl27/build/main.log || true

residual_risk: Focused Conclusion story polish and page-9 visual iteration only; this is not a final upload audit, target-policy refresh, or integrity-fingerprint refresh.
